QF.id: Securities Types Features and Asset Allocation Concepts_57

question.text: Capital gains bonds issued by entities such as NHAI, NABARD, NHB, IRFC, PFC, and REC allow investors to claim tax exemption on long-term capital gains under __________ of the Income Tax Act, 1961.

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Section 54EC of the Income Tax Act, 1961 provides exemption from long-term capital gains tax if the gains are invested in specified capital gains bonds issued by NHAI, NABARD, NHB, IRFC, PFC, and REC. These bonds have a lock-in period of 5 years and a maximum investment limit of Rs. 50 lakhs per financial year.
**The Trap:** Section 80C provides deductions for investments in PPF, ELSS, etc. -- it is the most commonly known tax-saving section but does not cover capital gains reinvestment. Section 10(15) provides interest income exemptions. Section 88 was an old rebate provision that has been discontinued.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Section 54EC bonds are tested in NISM Series XII and are relevant for tax planning questions in NISM V-A (Mutual Fund) and XV (Research Analyst).
**Key Takeaway:** Section 54EC = capital gains tax exemption by investing in specified bonds (NHAI, NABARD, NHB, IRFC, PFC, REC) with a 5-year lock-in and Rs. 50 lakh annual limit.
